---
title: "HPC in the Cloud"
subtitle: "Designing Quantum Computers using AWS"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
author: Cameron Rutherford
format:
  revealjs:
    output-ext: reveal.html
    theme: night
    smaller: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    menu: true
    chalkboard: false
    slide-level: 3
  html:
    toc: true
revealjs-plugins:
  - confetti
---

### Welcome!

- Spot instances, and chasing availability
  - Need to access two different internal dashboards
  - Worked to test / connect users with an MCP to chase instances
  - Ended up reverse engineering MCP calls to create function call for same thing
- Desktops, updating to Ubuntu
  - Needed to update since we needed newer glibc for VSCode
  - UX was different, but needed to update to use gnome and help users
- Issues with transferring workloads between regions:
  - Data / Simulations
  - Dotfiles / Home directories
- Security
  - Responding to incidents
  - Understanding claude code misconfiguration issues
  - Moving from static username / password to password less SSO
  - Security in the context of AI agents
  - VPC Network logging
- Improvements the system needed
  - Filesystem performance issues
  - Containerization and not using root for that
  - GCC updates for utilizing latest instances
  - Desktop load time improvements through pre-baking AMIs
  - Moving to latest version of code + decoupling scheduler from LDAP host
- User workflow improvements
- Getting them to use scratch storage
  - Minimizing number of jobs in the queue
  - Paladin - submit jobs from anywhere to compute backends across regions
  - AI
    - Skills for using the service / seeing availability
    - VSCode + Tmux for auto-reconnecting to long running sessions
    - Creating own personal agent memory / rules to better debug sessions
    - Slowly working with AI to debug complex live issues
- Infrastructure improvements
  - Building AMIs in one region, and transferring between them
  - Adding the proper return codes to build scripts so we can actually catch errors
  - Scaling tests to actually catch system regressions and verify fixes at scale
  - Preventing people overloaded the queue through default limits
